__data__.text: Public Works Commission Meeting 03-02-21 Item 4.A.

Public Works Commission Agenda Report

To: Chair Merrick and Members of the Public Works Commission Prepared by: Robert DuBoux, Public Works Director/City Engineer Date prepared:   February 18, 2021 Meeting date: March 2, 2021 Subject: Capital Improvement Projects and Disaster Recovery Projects Status Report
RECOMMENDED ACTION: Receive and file report on the status of the City’s current and upcoming Capital Improvements Projects and Disaster Recovery Projects.

DISCUSSION:  Staff will provide a status update on the following active projects in the Fiscal Year 2020-2021 Capital Improvement Program:

Capital Improvement Projects (Design)

Marie Canyon Green Streets

¾ Updates:

 The  City  has  recently  received  Measure  W  funds  to  complete  this project.

    Staff  is  working  on  the  project  plans  to  prepare  for  public bidding.

¾ Project Schedule:

 Complete Design: April 2021  Begin Construction: Summer 2021

PCH Median Improvements Project

¾ Updates:

 Consultant  is  working  on  the  final  design.

  In  March,  the  City  be seeking  proposals  from  consultants  to  perform  construction management.

¾ Project Schedule:  Complete Design: February 2021

 Begin Construction: Fall 2021

Project Schedule:  Complete Design: Spring 2021  Begin Construction:

Civic Center Water Treatment Facility Phase 2

¾ Updates:

 Project is at the 65% design phase.

  The  City  obtained  a  project  CDP  from  Planning  Commission  on February 1, 2021.

 The  State  is  reviewing  the  City’s  application  for  the  State  Revolving Fund program.

 The  project  assessment  engineer  has  begun  the  process  of evaluating the project costs.

 Next public community meeting is scheduled for March 25 th .

¾ Project Schedule:

 Complete Design: December 2021  Begin Construction: March 2022

Capital Improvement Projects (Construction)

City Hall Roof Replacement

¾ Updates:

  The  City  has  held  project  meetings  with  the  contractor  and  is developing a project schedule.

¾ Project Schedule: February – April 2021

Bluffs Park Workout Station

Bluffs Park Workout Station ¾ Updates:

  The  contractor  is  waiting  for  the  delivery  of  the  new  workout station equipment.  The equipment is anticipated to be delivered at the end of February.

¾ Project Schedule: November 2020 – March 2021

Civic Center Way Improvements

¾

Updates:  Work  Hours:  Monday  through  Friday  7:

00AM  to  4:00PM,  Saturdays 7:00AM to 4:00PM  The  contractor  is  currently  working  at  the  section  between  Vista Pacifica and the condos on Civic Center Way.

This phase of work will require  the  temporary  closure  of  Civic  Center  Way.

    Portions  of  the curb and gutter have been placed and the contactor is working on the proposed  retaining  wall.    This  portion  of  work  is  anticipated  to  be completed in early February 2021.

 The  contractor  is  also  working  on  the  curb,

  gutter  and  storm  drain installation on Civic Center Way from Webb Way to the condos.  This portion of the work may require temporary road closures.  However, it is  anticipated  that  this  phase  of  the  project  will  be  completed  at  the end of February 2021.

¾ Project Schedule: August 2020 through March 2021

Vehicle Protection Devices

¾ Project Description:  Per Malibu Municipal Code Section 17.48.

070 requires vehicle  impact  protection  devices  to  be  installed  for  all  parking  spaces located  adjacent  to  any  outdoor  pedestrian  seating  area.    There  are  two locations  within  the  City’s  right-of-way  and  City-owned  property  that  would require a vehicle impact protection device.

¾ Estimated Schedule:  Complete Design: Summer 2021

 Begin Construction: Fall 2021

Disaster Projects (Construction)

None at this time

Disaster Projects (Completed) Guardrail Replacement Citywide (FEMA/CalOES Project)

¾ Project  Description:

    This  project  consisted  of  replacing  the  damaged guardrail throughout the City as a result of the Woolsey Fire.

Corral Canyon Road Bridge Repairs (FEMA/CalOES Project)

¾ Project  Description:

    This  project  consisted  of  replacing  fire  damaged existing  fencing  and  repairing  the  damaged  embankment  adjacent  to  the bridge.Page 5 of 6
Agenda Item # 4.A.

Corral Canyon Culvert Repairs (FEMA/CalOES Project)

¾ Project Description:

  This project consisted of replacing a portion of Corral Canyon Road that was damaged from a failed storm drain.

Disaster Projects (Not Started) Clover Heights Storm Drain (FEMA Project)

¾ Project  Description:

    This  project  consists  of  design  and  construction  of  a few storm drain on Clover Heights.

The existing storm drain facility ends at the intersection of Clover Heights and Harvester Road.  During storms, this intersection floods and causes debris to block the road.  An extended storm drain towards the end of Clover Heights will help eliminate this issue.

¾ Estimated Schedule:

 Complete Design: Spring 2022

 Begin Construction: Summer 2022

Latigo Canyon Road Culvert Repairs (FEMA/CalOES Project)

¾ Project  Description:

    This  project  consists  of  repairing  the  existing  storm drain  on  Latigo  Canyon  Road  located  approximately  2,500  feet  from  PCH that was damaged by the Woolsey Fire.

¾ Estimated Schedule:

 Complete Design: Winter 2021

 Begin Construction: Spring 2022

Encinal Canyon Road Drainage Improvements (FEMA/CalOES Project)

¾ Project Description:

  This project consists of repairing damage storm drain facilities  and  roadway  embankments  that  were  damaged  by  the  Woolsey Fire.

¾ Estimated Schedule:

 Complete Design: Winter 2021

 Begin Construction: Spring 2022

Outdoor Warning Sirens (FEMA Project)

¾ Project Description:

  This project will be funded through a grant from FEMA after the Wooley Fire.  The project consists of hiring a consultant to develop a plan that includes the evaluation of a siren system and possible locations.

¾ Estimated Schedule:

 Complete Design: Dependent upon final grant approval from FEMA

Storm Drain Master Plan (FEMA Project)

¾ Project Description:

  This project will be funded through a grant from FEMA after the Woolsey Fire.  The City will create a complete inventory of storm drains,  culverts,  debris  basins,  manholes,  and  other  drainage  structures within the City.

¾ Estimated Schedule:

 Completion Date:  Spring 2022
